# UC-22.9.10 · Compliance Trending — Weighted Compliance Posture Composite and Driver Attribution

## Description

A single posture score communicates status to executives while driver attribution explains why it moved. Weighting domains (identity, data, resilience, third party) by materiality yields a composite that trends over time and complements—not replaces—framework-specific panels.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2, ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes compliance and sourcetypes posture:domain_score. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize `weight` to sum to 1 per week in ETL if not already; (2) simplify driver logic in production with explicit `driver_domain` from upstream model instead of `dedup` shortcut; (3) governance committee approves weights annually; (4) document formula in methodology PDF; (5) never use this score as sole regulatory metric. Indexes required: `index=compliance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=posture:domain_score`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect, `index=compliance` normalized score feeds.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="posture:domain_score"` (domain, score_0_100, weight, as_of_date).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize `weight` to sum to 1 per week in ETL if not already; (2) simplify driver logic in production with explicit `driver_domain` from upstream model instead of `dedup` shortcut; (3) governance committee approves weights annually; (4) document formula in methodology PDF; (5) never use this score as sole regulatory metric.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" earliest=-400d
| eval d=strptime(as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| bin d span=7d as week
| stats avg(score_0_100) as avg_score, latest(weight) as weight by week, domain
| eval contrib=avg_score*weight
| stats sum(contrib) as composite by week
| sort week
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(composite) as prev_composite
| eval wow_delta=round(composite-prev_composite,2)
| join type=left week [
    search index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" earliest=-400d
    | eval d=strptime(as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
    | bin d span=7d as week
    | stats avg(score_0_100) as s by week, domain
    | sort 0 week -s
    | dedup week
    | rename domain as top_driver_domain, s as top_driver_score
    | fields week, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score
  ]
| table week, composite, wow_delta, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score
```

Understanding this SPL

**Compliance Trending — Weighted Compliance Posture Composite and Driver Attribution** — A single posture score communicates status to executives while driver attribution explains why it moved. Weighting domains (identity, data, resilience, third party) by materiality yields a composite that trends over time and complements—not replaces—framework-specific panels.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="posture:domain_score"` (domain, score_0_100, weight, as_of_date). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect, `index=compliance` normalized score feeds. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance; **sourcetype**: posture:domain_score. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=compliance, sourcetype="posture:domain_score", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **d** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` aggregates events by **week, domain**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **contrib** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **week**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• `streamstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **wow_delta** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: avg_score, composite, contrib, d, domain, s, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (composite), Overlay (wow_delta), Single value (latest composite), Table (driver).

## SPL

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" earliest=-400d
| eval d=strptime(as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| bin d span=7d as week
| stats avg(score_0_100) as avg_score, latest(weight) as weight by week, domain
| eval contrib=avg_score*weight
| stats sum(contrib) as composite by week
| sort week
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(composite) as prev_composite
| eval wow_delta=round(composite-prev_composite,2)
| join type=left week [
    search index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" earliest=-400d
    | eval d=strptime(as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
    | bin d span=7d as week
    | stats avg(score_0_100) as s by week, domain
    | sort 0 week -s
    | dedup week
    | rename domain as top_driver_domain, s as top_driver_score
    | fields week, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score
  ]
| table week, composite, wow_delta, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score
```

## Visualization

Line chart (composite), Overlay (wow_delta), Single value (latest composite), Table (driver).
